trade.summary: Spagna and Israele have modest bilateral trade. Spagna exports food products, vehicles, and chemicals; Israele exports high-tech products, chemicals, and diamonds. The trade relationship is limited relative to both economies. Spagna has imposed arms embargo on Israele since October 2023.

diplomatic.summary: Spagna-Israele relations have deteriorated sharply since October 2023. Spagna under PM Sanchez has been among the EU's most vocal critics of Israele's Gaza operations. Spagna imposed an arms embargo, recognized Palestine as a state (May 2024) alongside Irlanda and Norvegia, and has called for ICJ investigations. Israele has responded with diplomatic protests and tensions are at post-war lows.
recent_incidents: Mutual diplomatic protests over Gaza war

military.summary: Military cooperation is suspended. Spagna imposed an arms embargo on Israele in 2023. Both are democracies but Spagna has taken a significantly adversarial diplomatic position. No joint exercises. The relationship is in the coolest state in decades.

financial.summary: Limited bilateral investment. Some Israelei tech companies have a Spanish presence. Spanish tourism to Israele has dropped sharply since October 2023. Financial ties are minimal and not growing under current diplomatic tensions.

energy.summary: Minimal bilateral energy trade. Spagna has not pursued Israelei offshore gas imports. No significant energy cooperation under current political climate.

scientific.summary: Historically moderate scientific cooperation, now under political strain. Spanish academic boycott calls have emerged post-October 2023. Prior collaboration in Mediterranean marine science, agritech, and life sciences. Scientific ties are at risk of further deterioration.
